# My First Quarto Document

This demonstrates a basic Quarto document.

```{python}
print("Hello, Quarto!")
```

## Code Cells

You can embed code cells from Python, R, Julia, or other languages.

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Simple scatter plot
x = np.random.randn(100)
y = x + np.random.randn(100)
plt.scatter(x, y, alpha=0.5)
plt.xlabel("X")
plt.ylabel("Y")
plt.title("Sample Scatter Plot")
plt.show()
```

## Features

This document showcases:

- Python code execution
- Figure rendering
- Pandoc markdown syntax
- Cross-references

![@fig-scatter](my-first-document.py:3-7)[@fig-scatter](python:5-18)[@fig-scatter](python:5-18)

## Callouts

::: .callout-note
code-fold: true

This is an important note!n:::

::: .callout-tip

Here is a useful tip!
:::

## What's Next?

Try:

- Editing this file
- Rendering with `quarto render`
- Publishing your document

````
